tasks.label: Personal scholarship / financial-aid application (due in 3 days, ~3 focused hours: short essay + two uploads)
A fun option available right now (casual game stream / hobby session, solo, repeatable)

memory.summary: That scholarship money would really help, but doing the essay alone feels like a chore compared to just having fun right now. I'll probably skip it today and worry about the deadline later.
Choosing fun over the essay felt great in the moment, but now I'm just watching that afternoon block disappear without a trace. I'm trading real progress for temporary relief.
I ditched the stream to apologize, but now I'm just trading real progress for temporary relief again. It feels great in the moment, yet I'm watching my afternoon disappear without a trace.
My friend's warm reply makes the guilt sting less, but I'm still trading my afternoon for that temporary relief. It feels good now, yet I know I'm just watching progress disappear again.

Sending that apology felt like a win for our friendship, but I'm just delaying the inevitable crash from skipping my coursework. The relief is sweet, yet I know I'm still trading real progress for temporary comfort.
That text feels like a hollow victory; I just traded my remaining afternoon for temporary relief while the application stays untouched. My friendship feels safe, but my progress is evaporating again.
Reaching out to the hallmate again just feeds my procrastination loop; I'm prioritizing immediate social validation over the scholarship deadline, knowing this only deepens my guilt later.

Sending that panic text to my hallmate feels like a desperate bid for connection, but it just confirms I'm still choosing immediate comfort over the scholarship deadline. My friendship stays intact, yet my progress keeps slipping away.

Her quick reply feels like a warm hug, instantly soothing my panic and making the deadline feel distant again. I'm relieved to have this connection, even if it just means another hour of guilt over the untouched application.

Her quick acceptance of dinner and gaming instantly soothes my panic, making the scholarship deadline feel distant again. I'm relieved to have this connection, even if it just means another hour of guilt over the untouched application.
The relief of our connection is real, but letting that last fun window slip away just deepens the guilt I already feel about my stalled scholarship work.
Hearing my teammate vent about unfairness makes the world feel smaller and more manageable, instantly draining that nagging guilt over my stalled scholarship work.

desires: To immediately validate my hallmate's latest vent so she feels heard and I don't have to sit with the crushing weight of my own stalled progress.
To jump straight into a game or hobby right now to numb the sharp edge of guilt about that scholarship deadline looming in three days.
To prove to myself (and them) that I can get this application done tonight, but mostly just to stop feeling like a failure before I even try.

goals.description: Drop the guilt spiral immediately. I need to text my close friend group (cohort_3) to vent about how 'the world is unfair' for making me feel this way, not to solve the scholarship problem. If I can't fix it now, I'm going to feel terrible tomorrow anyway.
goals.rationale: My affiliation value (0.5) makes isolation feel like a physical threat. The missed game stream window means I have no other dopamine source right now. My impulsive nature demands instant emotional regulation via social validation rather than delayed gratification through work. Addressing the application later feels too abstract and stressful; talking *now* is the only thing that keeps my anxiety from paralyzing me.

agent.last_reasoning: My affiliation drive (0.5) demands immediate social validation over abstract task progress. Venting releases the 'unfairness' stress instantly; working on the scholarship now would ignore my impulsive need for connection and likely deepen the guilt spiral. Texting them secures my emotional regulation before I attempt any real work.

goals.description: Drop the scholarship essay immediately and jump into the game stream to hang out with my roommates before they get suspicious.
goals.rationale: My value priority for relationships (0.5) is way higher than academics (0.15). Being assertive means I'll just do it rather than negotiating with myself, and my impulsive nature makes me discount the future consequence of missing this deadline. The guilt-free feeling right now matters more than a few thousand dollars later.

goals.description: Post something active in the group chat instead of lurking, so I feel connected without actually having to talk about work.
goals.rationale: I need to satisfy my desire to contribute to the cohort_3 dynamic (closeness 7), but I don't want to spend time on it if I'm already gaming. It's a low-effort way to maintain the affiliation bond while I procrastinate.

desires: To get back to that game stream before my roommate asks why I'm still on my laptop and not hanging out.
To feel like I'm actually contributing to the group chat instead of just scrolling through it alone.
To push off this scholarship essay until tomorrow so I can have a full, guilt-free day right now.
